questions.question: Where must the wildcard route { path: '**', ... } be placed in the routes array?
options: First — it must catch all routes before others
After the root route '/' only
Last — Angular matches routes in order; wildcard must not shadow specific routes
It doesn't matter — Angular automatically moves it to the end

questions.question: What does <router-outlet></router-outlet> do in an Angular template?
options: Creates a link to the router configuration
Defines where Angular renders the currently matched route's component
Enables routing in a standalone component
Creates a navigation menu from the routes array automatically

questions.question: How do you navigate to /users/42 using router.navigate()?
options: router.navigate('/users/42')
router.navigate(['/users', 42])
router.go('/users/42')
router.navigateTo({ path: 'users', id: 42 })

questions.question: What is the benefit of lazy loading a route with loadComponent?
options: The component renders faster because Angular pre-renders it
The component's JavaScript code is only downloaded when that route is first visited — reduces initial bundle
The component is cached after first load and never re-fetched
Angular automatically code-splits all components without needing loadComponent

questions.question: In UserDetailComponent, route.snapshot.paramMap.get('id') returns what type?
options: number
string | null
Observable<string>
ParamMap
